Create and maintain detailed functional specifications, configuration documents, test scripts, and user training materials. Conduct training sessions for end-users.

job_description:
As a Principal AI Architect, you will define and govern the end-to-end technical architecture of Makkook’s Optimization & Decision Intelligence platform(s), and ensure we can deliver new customer implementations fast (target: ≤ 8 weeks per use case after core platform maturity).
Core Responsibilities:
Platform Architecture & Technical Strategy
Define platform architecture: modular components, data contracts, integration patterns, deployment topology (cloud/on-prem), and security/observability standards.
Design reusable “platform primitives”: canonical entities (resources/tasks/time/cost), constraint packs, objective templates, scenario manager, and explainability layer.
Make solver/stack decisions (build vs buy), and establish technical roadmaps with the CTO.
Optimization & Decision Engine Leadership
Lead modeling and solution strategy for key families:
Production planning & scheduling (sequence-dependent setups/changeovers, minimum run lengths, maintenance windows)
Workforce allocation and shift planning
Routing, dispatching, and fleet assignment
Inventory/replenishment and supply chain planning
Set standards for model formulation, performance, scalability, and stability (runtime budgets, warm starts, decomposition, heuristics/hybrids where needed).
Build a repeatable benchmarking harness and KPI framework (cost, service, risk, feasibility rate, runtime).
Feasibility, Diagnostics & Explainability
Own the approach to infeasibility detection and repair (constraint relaxation strategies, IIS where applicable, actionable diagnostics).
Ensure decisions are explainable to operations teams (constraints hit, trade-offs, what changed vs previous plan, reason codes).
Delivery & Engineering Excellence
Partner with PM/Delivery to define scope, milestones, acceptance criteria, and technical risk management.
Guide integrations with ERP/WMS/TMS/CMMS/SCADA and production data pipelines.
Establish OptOps practices: versioning, monitoring, runtime regressions, rollback, and quality gates.
Technical Leadership
Mentor AI engineers (including those coming from NLP/CV backgrounds) into OR/Optimization craftsmanship.
Run architecture reviews, ADRs, and enforce best practices across code, models, and deployments.
job_requirements:
7+ years in applied optimization / operations research / decision intelligence, with hands-on delivery experience.
Strong track record in building and deploying optimization systems using one or more of:
MILP / MIP, CP/CP-SAT, decomposition, heuristics/metaheuristics, hybrid strategies.
Ability to translate real operations into formal models (capacities, time windows, setup/changeover, batching, minimum run lengths, resource calendars, SLAs).
Solid software engineering fundamentals for production systems (clean architecture, testing, CI/CD mindset, performance profiling).
Experience integrating decision engines into real enterprise environments (APIs, event-driven patterns, data pipelines, on-prem constraints).
Clear communication: can explain complex trade-offs to both engineers and operators.
Hands-on experience in: OR-Tools (CP-SAT), Gurobi/CPLEX, Pyomo, JuMP, or similar.
Experience with feasibility diagnostics (IIS, relaxation, reason codes, repair recommendations).
Experience in building “re-optimization loops” (daily/shift updates, warm starts, rolling-horizon planning).
Observability/monitoring: Prometheus/Grafana (or equivalent), structured logging, runtime regression monitoring.
Preferred to have domain exposure in manufacturing planning, logistics routing, retail replenishment, or supply chain optimization.

job_description:
Are you ready to power up your career with a dynamic role in Cairo, Egypt? We're on the hunt for a brilliant Electrical Tender Engineer to join our expanding team! If you possess a spark for innovation and a meticulous eye for detail, this full-time, on-site opportunity is your chance to make a significant impact in the Engineering - Mechanical/Electrical sector. We're looking for two talented individuals who are eager to grow and contribute from day one.
About the Role: Your Mission, Should You Choose to Accept
As an Electrical Tender Engineer, you will be a pivotal player in our pre-sales and project acquisition efforts. You'll dive deep into project requirements, craft winning proposals, and ensure our bids are not just competitive but also reflect our commitment to excellence. This role offers an incredible platform to develop your expertise and shape the future of electrical engineering projects.
Key Responsibilities: Igniting Innovation
Analyze Tender Documents: Meticulously review requests for proposals (RFPs), invitations to bid (ITBs), and other tender documents to understand project scope, specifications, and client requirements.
Cost Estimation & Analysis: Prepare detailed cost estimations for electrical components, systems, and installations, including materials, labor, and overheads.
Technical Proposal Development: Develop comprehensive and compelling technical proposals that clearly outline our proposed solutions, methodologies, and compliance with project specifications.
Supplier & Subcontractor Engagement: Coordinate with suppliers and subcontractors to obtain competitive quotations and ensure their offerings align with tender requirements.
Risk Assessment: Identify potential technical and commercial risks associated with tenders and propose mitigation strategies.
Collaboration: Work closely with sales, engineering, and project management teams to ensure a cohesive and winning bid strategy.
Documentation: Maintain accurate records of all tender-related correspondence, documentation, and submissions.
Skills & Technologies: Your Toolkit for Success
We're seeking candidates with a strong foundation in Electrical Engineering principles, coupled with practical experience in Tendering and Cost Estimation. Proficiency in tools like AutoCAD and the Microsoft Office Suite is essential. Your ability to engage in effective Communication, demonstrate keen Problem Solving, and uphold unwavering Attention to Detail will be key to your success.
Growth Opportunities: Electrify Your Career Path
At our company, your professional development is paramount. As an Entry Level professional, you'll benefit from mentorship, continuous learning, and exposure to a diverse range of projects. We provide clear pathways for advancement into senior engineering, project management, or specialized tendering roles. Your ambition will find fertile ground here!
Team & Culture: A Current of Collaboration
Join a vibrant, supportive, and highly collaborative team where your contributions are valued, and your ideas are heard. We foster an environment of mutual respect, continuous learning, and shared success. We believe in empowering our employees to achieve their full potential while working on challenging and rewarding projects in an on-site setting.
Impact: Be the Charge
Your work as an Electrical Tender Engineer will directly influence our growth and market position. You'll be instrumental in securing new projects, contributing to our reputation for excellence, and helping us deliver cutting-edge electrical solutions across various industries. Come be a part of something truly impactful!
job_requirements:
Educational Foundation: A Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering or a closely related field is highly desirable, providing a robust theoretical background.
Experience & Expertise: A minimum of 2-4 years of professional experience in Electrical Engineering, with a proven track record in Tendering and cost estimation.
Technical Prowess: Strong command of Electrical Engineering principles and practices. Proficiency with AutoCAD for technical drawings and layouts, and advanced skills in Microsoft Office Suite (especially Excel) for data analysis and proposal generation.
Critical Skills: Demonstrated ability in Cost Estimation, Proposal Writing, and interpreting Technical Specifications with meticulous Attention to Detail.
Communication Excellence: Outstanding Communication Skills, both written and verbal, essential for clear internal collaboration and compelling external proposals.
Problem Solving Acumen: A keen ability to identify challenges, analyze complex information, and devise effective solutions.
Career Level: This is an exciting opportunity for an Entry Level professional looking to significantly advance their career in a challenging yet rewarding environment.

job_description: The BIM Electrical Design Engineer is responsible for delivering electrical system designs using BIM as a platform for development, analysis, and coordination. The role focuses on power, lighting, ELV, and fire alarm system design across all project stages, ensuring compliance with international standards. BIM is utilized to enhance design accuracy, coordination, and documentation.
job_requirements:
Key Responsibilities:
* Develop electrical system designs from concept to IFC stage using BIM tools.
* Perform load calculations, demand estimation, and diversity analysis for buildings.
* Design complete electrical systems including:
   * LV power distribution (MDB, SMDB, DBs)
   * Cable sizing and voltage drop calculations
   * Earthing and bonding systems
   * Lightning protection systems
* Design lighting systems including:
   * Lux level calculations and fixture selection
   * Emergency lighting design
* Design ELV systems such as:
   * Data/telecom
   * CCTV
   * Access control
   * SMATV (as per project requirements)
* Design fire alarm systems in accordance with NFPA.
* Develop BIM models (LOD 200–300) as a design tool, ensuring accurate representation of calculated systems.
* Prepare and review:
   * Single line diagrams (SLD)
   * Load schedules
   * Cable routing layouts
   * Equipment layouts and details
   * Design reports and calculation sheets
* Ensure compliance with:
   * International standards (IEC, NFPA)
* Perform design coordination with mechanical, architectural, and structural disciplines.
* Conduct clash detection and resolve issues during design stages.
* Review contractor shop drawings and BIM models (LOD 400) to ensure compliance with design intent.
* Support tender documentation and respond to technical queries during bidding.
 Qualifications:
* Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering.
* 4–8 years of experience in electrical design within a consultancy environment.
* Experience in full project lifecycle (concept → IFC).
 Technical Skills:
* Strong knowledge of:
   * Electrical power distribution design
   * Lighting design and calculations
   * ELV and fire alarm systems
* Proficiency in:
   * Autodesk Revit MEP (as a design tool)
   * Dialux / Relux (lighting design)
   * ETAP or similar (load flow / short circuit – preferred)
   * Navisworks Manage (coordination)
* Good knowledge of:
   * IEC standards
   * NFPA codes
   * UAE authority regulations (DEWA, DCD, Etisalat/DU)
 Core Competencies:
* Strong engineering and analytical skills
* Ability to convert calculations into practical, buildable designs
* High attention to detail in design and BIM outputs
* Strong coordination skills across disciplines
* Ability to review and challenge contractor submissions effectively

The Technical Sales Engineer is responsible for driving revenue growth within the solar energy sector by actively generating new business opportunities, managing client relationships, and leading the full sales cycle. job_description:
About the Role
We are seeking an engineer who doesn't just "talk" to LLMs but builds autonomous, resilient systems. You will design multi-agent architecture that can reason, use tools, and recover from failures independently.
 Your primary goal is to bridge the gap between "cool demos" and "production-grade reliability." You will be responsible for deploying agents across various business sectors (e.g., Finance, Operations, Customer Success), ensuring they are not only intelligent but also safe, cost-effective, and predictable in a production environment.
 Key Responsibilities
 ●  Agent Orchestration: Design and implement stateful, multi-turn agent workflows using frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, PydanticAI, Swarm, Haystack, or Bee Agent Framework.
 ● Proactive Reliability & Guardrails: Architect systems that prevent production meltdowns. Implement circuit breakers, "human-in-the-loop" triggers, and input/output guardrails to stop infinite loops, prompt injections, and hallucinated tool calls before they reach the end user.
 ● Multi-Sector Tooling: Build and maintain high-precision API integrations (tools) that allow agents to interact with diverse business systems (ERPs, CRMs, custom databases) deterministically.
 ● Observability & Tracing: Set up advanced tracing (e.g., LangSmith, Langfuse, or Arize Phoenix) to debug complex reasoning chains and monitor agent trajectories in real-time.
 ● Rigorous Evaluation (Evals): Develop automated "Golden Datasets" and evaluation frameworks (using RAGas, DeepEval, G-Eval, LangSmith Evaluators, or custom model-based grading) to measure agent success rates and prevent regressions before every deployment.
 ● Cost & Latency Optimization: Manage the "token budget" by implementing tiered model routing (e.g., using Gemini 3 Flash for initial reasoning and Ultra for final verification) and optimizing context window usage.
job_requirements:
Required Qualifications
 ●  3+ years of Software Engineering experience, with a background in Python and FastAPI (or similar).
 ● 1+ year of experience specifically focused on LLM-based Agents in a production environment.
 ● Deep Expertise in Agentic Patterns: Proven experience implementing ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, or Multi-Agent Supervisor architectures.
 ● Production Infrastructure: Hands-on experience with modern Vector Databases and Search Engines (e.g., Pinecone, Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant, Chroma, or pgvector) and semantic caching strategies.
 ● Robustness Mindset: A track record of handling non-deterministic failures (e.g., implementing custom retry logic and fallback strategies for failed tool calls).
 Preferred Qualifications (The "Plus" Factors)
 ● Experience with Fine-tuning smaller models (e.g., Llama 3) for specific tool-calling tasks to reduce latency.
 ● Knowledge of Data Privacy (handling PII in agent prompts) and AI Security best practices.
 ● Prior experience building agents for specialized sectors like Fintech or Supply Chain.

job_title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer Backend · Frontend · DevOps · Cloud Infrastructure
job_description:
ABOUT THE ROLE
DimCarpi GmbH is a Munich-based lifestyle and fashion brand — registered under HRB 304755 — that delivers elegant, functional apparel and accessories to a global audience. As we scale our digital infrastructure, we are building a world-class engineering team in Egypt.
 We are looking for a Senior Full-Stack Engineer who is genuinely strong across the entire stack: from crafting pixel-perfect, high-performance frontends to designing fault-tolerant backend services and deploying them on modern cloud infrastructure. This is a high-impact, high-ownership role. Your code will be running in production, at scale, from day one.
 You should care deeply about system reliability, security, and developer experience — not just feature delivery. If you enjoy solving hard infrastructure and architecture problems as much as writing clean application code, this role is built for you.
 KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Backend Engineering
Design, build, and maintain production-grade RESTful and GraphQL APIs using Node.js frameworks (Fastify, Express.js, NestJS) or Go (Gin/Echo) or Python (FastAPI)
Architect and implement microservices and event-driven systems that are decoupled, observable, and resilient
Manage database design, migrations, indexing strategies, and query optimization across PostgreSQL and MongoDB
Implement robust authentication and authorization systems — OAuth 2.0, JWT, RBAC, session management
Write well-tested, well-documented backend code with a focus on maintainability and long-term scalability
Frontend Engineering
Build high-performance, accessible frontend applications using React, Next.js (App Router / Server Components), Angular, or Vue.js / Vite
Apply modern state management patterns (Zustand, Redux Toolkit, React Query / TanStack Query)
Ensure excellent Core Web Vitals: optimize for LCP, FID, CLS, and TTI across all pages
Implement responsive, accessible UI using Tailwind CSS or component libraries (MUI, Radix UI)
Collaborate closely with product and design to translate wireframes into production-ready interfaces
Infrastructure & DevOps
Containerize services using Docker and orchestrate workloads with Kubernetes (K8s)
Deploy and operate applications on cloud platforms — AWS (EC2, ECS, RDS, S3, CloudFront, Lambda), Hetzner Cloud, or DigitalOcean / Vultr
Configure and optimize NGINX as a reverse proxy, load balancer, and TLS termination point
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar tools
Set up monitoring, alerting, and distributed tracing using Prometheus, Grafana, Sentry, or Datadog
Manage infrastructure as code using Terraform or Pulumi
Performance, Security & Reliability
Identify and eliminate performance bottlenecks across frontend (bundle size, render blocking, lazy loading) and backend (N+1 queries, slow endpoints, inefficient caching)
Implement multi-layer caching strategies: Redis (in-memory), CDN (CloudFront, Cloudflare), and HTTP cache headers
Apply security best practices: input validation and sanitization, protection against OWASP Top 10 (XSS, CSRF, SQL injection, IDOR), secrets management, and dependency auditing
Design for high availability: implement retries, circuit breakers, graceful degradation, health checks, and automated failover

Job Description
You will lead the technical hydraulic and hydrological work across a mixed portfolio of infrastructure design, feasibility studies, and donor-funded programs. Working directly with the Technical Director, you own the modelling, design, and technical writing from raw data to the final deliverable.
Your core work will cover hydrological modelling for catchment analysis, rainfall-runoff, flood frequency, and water balance studies; hydraulic modelling of rivers, open channels, and urban drainage systems; 1D/2D flood modelling and flood hazard mapping; water distribution and drinking water network design and analysis; stormwater and sewer network modelling for urban drainage and master planning; and detailed engineering design for water supply schemes, drainage works, flood protection, and water harvesting infrastructure.
You will produce engineering drawings, write technical reports and design notes to a professional standard, and contribute to feasibility studies and EIA technical inputs on water-related components.
Beyond delivery, you will play an active role in business development leading the technical methodology, work plan, and experience sections of proposals and tenders, and supporting the continuous development of the firm's credentials library.

Proficiency in conducting Safety Audits, leading Incident Investigation, and developing robust Emergency Response Planning are essential. You will leverage your understanding of Occupational Health, Environmental Protection, and rigorous Safety Regulations Compliance to safeguard our projects. Strong Effective Communication and Leadership skills are paramount to drive our safety culture.

We are committed to nurturing your career through challenging projects, specialized training, and mentorship opportunities. As you grow with us, you'll have the chance to take on greater responsibilities and influence the future of our safety practices across the region.

We foster an environment of open communication, mutual respect, and shared responsibility. Your contributions will be celebrated, and your voice heard, as we collectively strive for incident-free operations.

Be a part of something truly significant, where your work leaves a lasting, positive legacy.

The QC Supervisor will be responsible for overseeing and ensuring quality compliance across commercial and construction projects within the organization’s engineering and facility development operations.
The role involves supervising inspection activities, monitoring construction quality standards, ensuring compliance with project specifications and regulatory requirements, and coordinating with project teams to maintain high-quality execution throughout all project phases.
 Key Responsibilities
Supervise and implement Quality Control systems, policies, and procedures across construction and commercial projects.
Conduct regular site inspections to ensure compliance with project specifications, engineering drawings, and quality standards.
Review materials, workmanship, and installation processes to ensure adherence to project quality requirements.
Identify non-conformities and lead corrective and preventive action plans.
Coordinate with Project Managers, Site Engineers, and Contractors to maintain quality standards during project execution.
Prepare and maintain QC documentation, reports, and inspection records.
Ensure compliance with relevant ISO standards, construction regulations, and safety requirements.
Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance quality performance across projects.

job_description:
Key Responsibilities
Security Strategy & Governance
Develop, implement, and maintain the organization's information security strategy, policies, and standards aligned with business objectives and regulatory requirements (ISO 27001, NIST, HIPA).

Risk Management
Identify, assess, and priorities information security risks. Maintain the risk register and drive remediation plans in collaboration with stakeholders across all business units.
Security Operations Oversight
Oversee day-to-day SOC operations including SIEM monitoring, incident triage, threat intelligence, and EDR management to ensure timely detection and response to threats.
Incident Response & Crisis Management
Lead the response to security incidents, breaches, and crises. Own the Incident Response Plan, conduct post-incident reviews, and drive continuous improvement of detection and response capabilities.
Compliance & Audit
Ensure adherence to applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations. Manage internal and external audits, track findings to closure, and maintain compliance evidence documentation.
Vulnerability & Penetration Testing
Define and oversee the vulnerability management lifecycle. Commission and review penetration testing engagements, priorities remediation, and report on security posture trends to executive leadership.
Security Awareness & Culture
Design and deliver security awareness training programs. 
 Champion a security-first culture across the organization and communicate risk clearly to non-technical audiences.
Team Leadership & Development
Lead, mentor, and develop the security team. Define KPI frameworks, conduct performance reviews, manage workloads, and build talent pipelines to support organizational growth.
Security Architecture & Projects
Provide security guidance on new projects, system designs, and technology adoptions. Collaborate with IT and engineering teams to embed security by design into all initiatives.
Reporting & Executive Communication
Produce regular security dashboards, board-level risk reports, and KPI metrics. Translate complex technical risk into clear business impact narratives for senior leadership.

job_description:
Senior Odoo Developer — Integrations & Financial Reporting
About the Project
We are a digital agency (Anecdote) with our in-house development team, running Odoo as the operational hub for project management, campaign workflows, and finance. We maintain custom modules that link external advertising and analytics platforms to Odoo — automating budget tracking, media planning, and performance reporting — and extend Odoo's accounting layer for localised financial statements, asset management, and bank reconciliation. The stack is cloud-hosted in a containerised deployment with CI/CD automation.
 What You'll Work On
 Third-party integrations: Maintain and extend connections to advertising and analytics platforms via REST/JSON APIs and their SDKs — handling data sync, TTL-based caching, and robust error handling.
Campaign & project workflows: Build and evolve wizards and automation that translate campaign planning data into structured Odoo work items (tasks, budgets, timelines) and generate QWeb reports with KPI summaries.
Budget & time tracking: Develop models and logic for rolling budget computation, variable service pricing, and time allocation across hierarchical task structures.
Accounting & financial reporting: Extend core Odoo accounting with additional reports (PDF and Excel), financial statements, asset tracking flows, and reconciliation support.
Custom views & OWL components: Create and maintain Kanban/List view extensions, field widgets, and OWL components — including custom styling where needed.
Platform & CI/CD: Work within a Docker-based local dev environment and a CI pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to a container registry on merge.
Test coverage: Champion automated testing — writing model-level and integration tests to establish a growing safety net across the codebase.
 Tech Stack
 ERP: Odoo
Backend: Python, Odoo ORM
Frontend: OWL (Odoo Web Library), JavaScript, SCSS
External APIs: Advertising/marketing platform APIs
Database: PostgreSQL
Reporting: QWeb (PDF), openpyxl (Excel)
Containerisation: Docker, Docker Compose
CI/CD: Bitbucket Pipelines, container registry
 How We Work
 Module-first architecture: every feature lives in its own addon with clean __manifest__.py dependency declarations — new work follows the same pattern.
Small, focused team: compact dev team where each engineer owns full vertical slices (model → view → report → deployment).
Docker-native workflow: local dev runs entirely in containers via Docker Compose; there is no bare-metal Odoo setup.
CI on merge: builds are image-cached to keep pipeline times short; deployments are triggered automatically on merge.
Growing test culture: we want someone who will help establish and expand automated test coverage — this is an active priority, not an afterthought.
Code style: consistent use of Odoo decorator patterns and access-control matrices.
job_requirements:
Must-Have Qualifications
 3+ years of Odoo development.
Python ORM mastery: computed/related fields with @api.depends, @api.[removed], @api.constrains, _sql_constraints, and CRUD overrides (create, write, unlink).
Model inheritance patterns: _inherit (extension), abstract mixins, and TransientModel wizards.
Security model: authoring ir.model.access.csv, record rules (ir.rule), group-based permissions, and knowing when (and when not) to use sudo().
QWeb reports: designing and debugging XML report templates that produce both PDF and structured data output.
External API integration: consuming REST/JSON APIs from Python with proper error handling; hands-on experience with at least one third-party SDK.
Odoo JS ecosystem: custom OWL components, extending standard Kanban/List controllers, understanding the asset bundle pipeline.
Docker: comfortable writing and debugging docker-compose.yml files; able to trace build failures through multi-stage Dockerfile logic.
 Nice-to-Have
 Major ad platform APIs (e.g. Meta, Tiktok, Snapchat) — existing familiarity with advertising platform APIs shortens onboarding significantly.
Accounting domain knowledge — understanding of double-entry bookkeeping, asset depreciation schedules, and bank reconciliation flows.
AWS & Bitbucket Pipelines — experience with cloud container registries and pipeline YAML configuration is a plus.
openpyxl / xlsxwriter — used for Excel report exports; ability to produce formatted multi-sheet workbooks.
Bank statement parsing — familiarity with OFX/QIF formats or relevant parsing libraries.
PostgreSQL performance tuning — the reporting layer will benefit from query-level optimisation as data grows.
pytest-odoo or Odoo test runner — experience writing TransactionCase and HttpCase tests; bootstrapping a test suite from scratch is highly valued.

job_description:
We are seeking a highly experienced RFID Solutions Architect & Implementation Lead to design, deploy, and optimize RFID systems across our library infrastructure. This role combines strategic system architecture, hands-on technical implementation, and project leadership.
The ideal candidate will lead the end-to-end RFID deployment for library and archival environments, including system design, hardware configuration, software integration, and operational optimization. You will work closely with IT teams, engineers, and library stakeholders to ensure seamless integration with existing systems and deliver a reliable RFID-enabled ecosystem.

*Key Responsibilities*
 RFID System Architecture & Design
Design the complete RFID system architecture for library environments including security gates, self-check stations, handheld scanners, and RFID detection systems.
Conduct site surveys and technical assessments to determine optimal placement of RFID hardware.
Define technical specifications for RFID components including tags, readers, antennas, controllers, and middleware.
Develop scalable solutions that support large collections and multi-site library deployments.
 Implementation & Deployment
Lead the installation, configuration, and commissioning of RFID systems across library locations.
Configure RFID tags and ensure proper tagging strategies for books, manuscripts, and archival materials.
Implement RFID hardware including gates, readers, antennas, handheld devices, and detectors.
Perform system testing, troubleshooting, and performance optimization.
System Integration
Integrate RFID systems with Library Management Systems (LMS) and enterprise platforms.
Ensure compatibility with databases and enterprise systems such as ERP and Business Applications.
Support customization and system development using programming languages such as C# or Java when required.
Ensure reliable data exchange between RFID systems and backend databases.
Technical Support & Training
Provide technical guidance and operational training to library and IT staff.
Develop detailed technical documentation, system diagrams, and operational procedures.
Provide ongoing system monitoring, maintenance, and troubleshooting support.
Project Management
Develop and manage a comprehensive RFID implementation roadmap.
Coordinate with stakeholders, vendors, and technical teams throughout the project lifecycle.
Track project KPIs, timelines, and deliverables to ensure successful deployment.
Identify project risks and implement mitigation strategies.
Standards & Compliance
Ensure compliance with international RFID standards including ISO/IEC 18000 and EPCglobal.
Follow industry best practices for RFID implementation, security, and system reliability.

Technical proficiency in system integration and software customization.

The Hospital Event & Engagement Coordinator is the primary person responsible for the end-to-end lifecycle of all hospital-related gatherings. Identify, contact, and negotiate with external event providers and organizers.

Perform full technical setup, including configuring audiovisual (AV) tools, microphones, projectors, and other digital devices.
Review all presentations (PowerPoint, etc.) prior to events to ensure technical compatibility and content quality.
Oversee catering arrangements for both indoor and outdoor settings, ensuring food safety and dietary compliance.

Coordinate with internal hospital departments to ensure events do not interfere with patient care.

Independently execute competitions, festivals, and holiday celebrations for staff and patients.
Source and choose meaningful gifts for speakers, retirees, or contest winners.
Personally package, wrap, and prepare gifts to ensure a professional presentation.
Serve as the "Master of Ceremonies" to introduce and distribute gifts and awards during live ceremonies.

job_description:
Are you a visionary E-Commerce Specialist ready to make a significant impact? We are seeking a dynamic and experienced professional to join our team in the vibrant city of Cairo, Egypt. This is an incredible on-site opportunity to drive our digital presence and revolutionize our online sales strategies. If you're passionate about leveraging digital channels to create unparalleled customer experiences and achieve ambitious sales targets, then this is your moment to shine!
Overview: As our E-Commerce Specialist, you will be at the forefront of our digital commerce initiatives, shaping the future of our online brand. You will be instrumental in expanding our reach, optimizing our platforms, and converting digital interactions into tangible growth. We are looking for a creative strategist with a strong analytical mind, ready to take ownership and deliver exceptional results.
Key Responsibilities:
Strategize & Execute: Develop and implement comprehensive e-commerce strategies to maximize online sales and profitability.
Platform Management: Oversee the day-to-day operations and continuous optimization of our e-commerce platforms, ensuring a seamless user experience.
Digital Campaigns: Plan, execute, and monitor compelling digital marketing and social media campaigns to drive traffic, engagement, and conversions.
Performance Analysis: Utilize robust analytics tools to track KPIs, identify trends, and provide actionable insights for continuous improvement.
Content & Merchandising: Work closely with marketing and product teams to ensure engaging content, effective product merchandising, and compelling promotions.
Customer Journey: Optimize the entire customer journey from awareness to post-purchase, ensuring an exceptional brand experience.
Skills & Technologies: You will be a master of Commerce and E-Marketing, with a deep understanding of modern Marketing principles and sophisticated Social Media strategies. Proficiency in SEO, SEM, Content Marketing, and developing a robust Digital Marketing Strategy is essential. Experience with Google Analytics and various E-commerce Platform Management systems is crucial, alongside expertise in Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO), strong Sales Acumen, and excellent Communication and Data Analysis skills.
Growth Opportunities: This role offers immense potential for professional development in a rapidly evolving market. You'll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge technologies, contribute to strategic decision-making, and have the opportunity to grow into senior leadership roles as our company expands. We believe in investing in our talent and fostering a culture of continuous learning and innovation.
Team & Culture: Join a dynamic, collaborative, and forward-thinking team that thrives on creativity and results. Our on-site environment in Cairo encourages open communication, shared success, and a supportive atmosphere where every team member's contribution is valued. We celebrate innovation and empower our employees to bring their best ideas to the table.
Impact: Your work will directly influence our market presence and sales performance. You will play a critical role in enhancing our brand's visibility, driving customer loyalty, and contributing significantly to our overall business growth and success in the competitive e-commerce landscape.

Experience That Excites: Possess an impressive 3-7 years of progressive experience in e-commerce, digital marketing, or a closely related field, demonstrating a strong track record of driving online sales and digital growth.
Master of Digital Commerce: Exhibit a profound understanding and practical application of Commerce, E-Marketing, and overarching Marketing principles.
Social Media Savvy: Proven expertise in leveraging Social Media platforms for brand building, engagement, and direct sales.
SEO & SEM Guru: Strong command of SEO and SEM best practices, with a demonstrated ability to improve organic and paid search performance.

You'll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge ERP systems, contribute to vital financial reporting, and become a key player in our accounting department.
Key Responsibilities:
Execute daily accounting tasks, including processing invoices, managing accounts payable and receivable, and performing bank reconciliations.
Support the preparation of financial statements and reports, ensuring compliance with accounting standards.
Maintain accurate and up-to-date general ledger accounts, ensuring all transactions are recorded correctly.
Utilize Odoo ERP extensively to streamline accounting processes and optimize financial data management.
Assist with month-end and year-end close procedures, contributing to timely and accurate financial reporting.
Collaborate with internal teams to resolve discrepancies and provide financial insights.

Our core system is Odoo ERP, so prior experience with Odoo is a significant advantage. Naturally, expertise in Microsoft Office, especially Microsoft Excel, is essential for success in this role.
Growth Opportunities:
At our company, your professional development is paramount. We offer a clear pathway for career advancement, continuous learning opportunities, and mentorship from seasoned professionals. Grow your expertise in Financial Reporting, master General Ledger Management, and develop advanced Problem-Solving skills that will serve you throughout your career.

Join a collaborative and supportive team that values innovation, integrity, and mutual respect. We foster an environment where every voice is heard, and teamwork is celebrated. You'll be part of a dynamic group committed to achieving financial excellence and making a real impact.
Impact:
As a Junior Accountant, you'll play a direct role in maintaining the financial health and operational efficiency of our organization. Your dedication to accuracy and your proactive approach will contribute significantly to our strategic decision-making and overall success in the market.

job_title: Bilingual Italian and English Senior Sales Advisor
job_description:
Job Details
Be the spark that brightens days and ignite your career with TTEC’s award-winning employment experience. As a Italian - English Senior Sales Representative working on site in Cairo, Egypt, you’ll be a part of creating and delivering amazing customer experiences while you also #experienceTTEC, an award-winning employment experience and company culture.
What You’ll be Doing
Your role as a Senior Sales Advisor will involve generating new business, contacting existing customers, and using a consultative approach to provide tailored solutions that meet the clients’ unique needs, while executing sales strategies to achieve the targets through both outbound and inbound sales calls.  
During a Typical Day, You’ll
Grow your book of business by developing new business opportunities, establishing and nurturing customer relationships and ensuring retention.
Getting through to the decision maker by utilizing the Influencers and Users to help close the business.
Engage customers in a consultative manner to develop value propositions that cater to their needs, with the goal of increasing volume for existing clients and acquiring volume for new customers.
To respond to incoming calls/emails and ensure the delivery of outstanding customer experience.
Identify the customer’s shipping profile to secure their business or alternatively, refer the lead to the appropriate channel.
A Bit More About Your Role
We’ll train you to be a subject matter expert in your field, so you can be confident in providing the highest level of service possible whether through voice, chat or email interactions. We trust you already have the necessary ingredient that can’t be taught – a caring and supportive nature that will shine through as you help customers. You’ll also have a chance to make great new friends within the TTEC community and grow your career in a dynamic, family-friendly atmosphere.
You'll report to Sales Director. You’ll contribute to the success of the customer experience as well as the overall success of the team.
About TTEC
Our business is about making customers happy. That’s all we do. Since 1982, we’ve helped companies build engaged, pleased, profitable customer experiences powered by our combination of humanity and technology. On behalf of many of the world’s leading iconic and disruptive brands, we talk, message, text, and video chat with millions of customers every day. These exceptional customer experiences start with you.
TTEC is proud to be an equal opportunity employer where all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. TTEC embraces and is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce that respects and empowers the culture and perspectives within our global teams. We strive to reflect the communities we serve by not only delivering amazing service and technology, but also humanity. We make it a point to make sure all our employees feel valued and comfortable being their authentic selves at work. As a global company, we know diversity is our strength. It enables us to view projects and ideas from different vantage points and allows every individual to bring value to the table in their own unique way.
job_requirements:
What You Bring to the Role
A proven track record in B2B sales
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to adapt to different international cultures
Demonstrated proficiency in sales negotiation and closing techniques.
Adopt a well-rounded approach that includes cold calling, relationship building, sales process and appointment setting.
Goal-oriented with a strong focus on achieving targets and contributing to business growth.
The ability to understand and share the feelings of customers seeking assistance and remedies.
Proficiency in Italian at the C1 minimum level and proficiency in English at a minimum of B2 level.
What You Can Expect
Excellent sales, product and systems training are provided with ongoing sales coaching.
Knowledgeable, encouraging, supporting and present leadership.
Diverse and community minded organization
Career-growth and lots of learning opportunities for aspiring minds
Competitive remuneration package, monthly bonus based on performance and 25 days paid leave.
Private medical & life insurance plan and Tuition Reimbursement

job_title: English speaking - Senior Customer Service Representative
job_description:
Job Details
Be the spark that brightens days and ignite your career with TTEC’s award-winning employment experience. As a CSenior Customer Service Representative - English speaking - Nights – supporting Volkswagen (VWG)working in our Cairo location, you’ll be a part of creating and delivering amazing customer experiences for Volkswagen's business customers while you also #ExperienceTTEC, an award-winning employment experience and company culture.  You’ll be a part of bringing humanity to business. #experienceTTEC
What You’ll be Doing
Do you have a passion for helping others? In this role, you’ll support retailers for Volkswagen, the leading multi-branded motor manufacturer. Whether it’s getting answers for customers quickly, consulting on products or resolving their issues with a smile, you’ll be the difference between their customer experience being just average or an exceptional one.  You’ll receive inbound contacts across multiple channels from retailers across our client’s brands. You’ll make outbound contacts where required and work with third parties in resolving retailer questions and business process related cases. You’ll build effective and efficient working relationships with key client contacts within relevant networks and with external and internal third parties. 
During a Typical Day, You’ll
Providing customer service to VW dealerships and retailers 
Be a VW brand advocate  
Liaise with other suppliers such vehicle logistics companies, port authorities, IT support desk, and external fulfilment operations 
Keep up to date with products, pricing, marketing, business developments and procedures 
Highlight issues, trends, or areas of opportunity for improvement of services 
 What You Can Expect 
Supportive of your career and professional development
An inclusive culture and community minded organization where giving back is encouraged
A global team of curious lifelong learners guided by our company values
Base wage plus performance bonus opportunities
And we have a healthy benefits package based on your position that could include PTO, tuition reimbursement, health and wellness incentives
 A Bit More About Your Role
We’re committed to ensuring you have the skills and support to be successful in your role throughout your career. From your first day in training, through individualized webcam-enabled, engagement and coaching, on into 1000s of free courses to support your career growth wherever that may take you. And we know that you bring with you the one necessary ingredient that can’t be taught – a caring and supportive nature that will shine through as you help customers. Our TTEC community is here for you as one dynamic, global family.
You'll report to a Team Lead. You'll contribute to the success of the customer experience and the overall success of the team.
About TTEC
TTEC is a global consulting, technology, and managed services company that helps brands deliver smarter, more human customer experiences through data, AI, and technology. Our work connects people and brands every day—and it starts with the talent behind the experience.
TTEC is proud to be an equal opportunity employer where all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. TTEC embraces and is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce that respects and empowers the cultures and perspectives within our global teams. We aim to reflect the communities we serve, by not only delivering amazing service and technology, but also humanity. We make it a point to make sure all our employees feel valued, belonging, and comfortable being their authentic selves at work. As a global company, we know diversity is our strength because it enables us to view things from different vantage points and for you to bring value to the table in your own unique way.
job_requirements:
What You Bring to the Role
Experience supporting business processes, client handling skills within an automotive retail environment or with a strong interest or working knowledge of cars / commercial vehicles and the supporting retailer service Networks
Client focused and resolution-oriented mindset
Strong written communication skills
Ability to demonstrate negotiation, conflict management and empathy skills
Computer savvy

job_description:
Liaising with clients and other engineers to ensure projects are completed according to specifications.
Preparation/Review of commissioning plans, commissioning program and commissioning activity trackers.
Preparing/Review Operation and maintenance manuals, Training manuals and Asset registers.
Prepare/Review testing and commissioning method statements and procedures as per the manufacturer recommendations, project specification and international standards.
Review the design drawings and highlight commission ability issues.
Prepare the progress report as per the site activity and highlight the issues effecting the commissioning process.
Prepare a design review report for electrical systems including the design calculations review and drawings review.
Perform a complete design package for tender process including preparation of design electrical drawings (Power, lighting, lighting control, ELV systems, installation details, earthing and lightning protection) in coordination with other trades.
Indoor and outdoor lighting design, distribution and calculation using DIALUX software as per the relevant codes and building requirements.
Preparation of Panel Board Schedules, MCC, calculations sheets, Load estimations, cables tables, equipment schedules as per the relevant codes and building requirements.
Substations builder work, equipment arrangement and cable routing design.
Voltage Drop and Short Circuit calculations manually and using ETAP and ECODIAL.
Preparation and development of Electrical and ELV specifications, and conduct design reports.
Preparation and development of projects OPR and BODR.
Risk assessment and calculations for Lightning protection and Earthing.
Low and Medium Voltage infrastructure networks design including Internal and external layouts, wiring diagrams, power riser, single line diagram.
Interpret engineering drawings for control panels and LV panels.
Extra low voltage systems design including but not limited to: Structure cabling, Fire alarm, CCTV, Wi Fi, Access, TV, Telephone, Sound, Intercom, Triple play, BMS, Fiber Optics telecommunications systems, PAVA etc
Review Shop drawings and As-built drawings.
Interpret Catalogs, design brief and criteria, installation manuals and datasheets. 
Review of vendor drawings, inspection considering testing of vendor's works and as built drawings.
Review of technical, financial offers, suppliers’ technical submittals, equipment manuals in accordance with the electrical design package.
Preparation of bill of quantities, ensuring the consistency of BOQ with specifications.
Analyzing existing processes and scheduling meetings to discuss improvement initiatives.
Manage the technical office engineers to make sure the technical documents are being prepared with the highest quality.
Evaluating project requirements and proposing innovative design alternatives to reduce costs and increase efficiency.
Supervise, lead and train junior engineers to ensure their technical development goals are met.
Interacting with the Management & Contractors / Supervisors for the quality and service problems.
Liaise with electrical authorities for the approval of service connection.
job_requirements:
Bachelor of Electrical Engineering – power and machines major.
Excellent knowledge of international codes and standards, Saudi Arabian electrical and Civil Defense standards.
5-7 years of Proven experience in electrical systems design and project management.
Proficiency in CAD software and other relevant engineering tools.
Proficiency in using Dialux and ETAP softwares.
Very good command of Revit MEP Software.
Proficiency in using Microsoft Office.
Very Good English Language spoken and writing skills.
Familiarity with value engineering concepts and practices.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.

skills: ['HSSE', 'Health and Safety', 'Food and Beverage', 'Risk Assessment', 'HSE', 'HSE Management', 'Engineering', 'Health, Safety and Environment']
job_description:
Promote good health, safety and environmental practices throughout all Company activities.
Communicate HSE procedures and standards to subcontractors.
Establish and maintain Safety manual and employee booklet.
Review the Company Health, Safety and Environment Policy Statements on an annual basis.
Ensure that Local Policy Statements/Fire Plans and Fire Risk Assessments are produced and quality assured.
Advise, develop and monitor effective Risk Assessment management strategies and make recommendations on the implementation of these strategies.
Review and develop health and safety policies, procedures and guidance, in keeping with best Practice and make recommendations on their implementation and application.
Consult with senior managers and other line managers and give appropriate advice on formulating best-practice policies and procedures.
Provide where necessary source, training for staff in relation to Health and Safety policies, practice and procedures.

The Media Buyer works cross-functionally with the Account Manager and Digital Marketing Strategist to ensure campaigns meet business goals and are delivered with accuracy and speed.

Support the Content & Media Management team by aligning paid media execution with the monthly content calendar.
Provide media performance feedback to inform SEO blog and newsletter optimization.
Coordinate closely with strategist and social media team on promotional content planning.
8. Ad Spend Reporting & Coordination

Support the Account Manager and Accountant with campaign pauses and resume actions during delayed payment cycles.

Overview: As our Network Engineer, you will be the backbone of our digital operations, responsible for the design, implementation, and ongoing management of our critical network systems. Engineer and deploy resilient network solutions, including LAN/WAN, VPN, and data center networks, ensuring scalability and high availability.

A solid understanding of TCP/IP Protocols, VPN Technologies, and LAN/WAN Architecture is essential. Growth Opportunities: We are committed to your professional development. You will have access to continuous learning resources, opportunities to pursue advanced industry certifications, and the chance to lead impactful projects. We foster an environment where your skills will evolve with emerging technologies, keeping you at the forefront of network engineering innovations.
Team & Culture: Join a collaborative, innovative, and highly skilled team that values knowledge sharing and mutual support. We believe in empowering our engineers to take ownership, propose creative solutions, and contribute directly to our success. Expect a supportive culture that champions continuous learning and professional growth.
Impact: Your work will be critical in ensuring the stability, security, and lightning-fast performance of our entire technological backbone. You will directly contribute to our operational excellence and enable our business to thrive in a rapidly evolving digital landscape. This role offers the chance to leave a lasting, positive mark on our infrastructure and our future.

Talent Acquisition (60%)
Lead full‑cycle recruitment for all roles and high-volume hiring when required: sourcing, screening, interviewing, offer negotiation, and onboarding coordination.

job_description:
The problem
Energy Market is the big brother to the stock market and it has similar trading mechanisms and similar forecasting problems and we are building the first AI energy trader that makes energy trading more accessible and profitable
Energy prices change every few minutes. Batteries can profit from that volatility, buying low, selling high, and getting paid to stabilize the grid, but only if the trading decisions are faster and smarter than any human can manage.
That’s what we’re building at Solship: an AI that trades energy autonomously, 24/7. Our models run millions of simulations per minute to decide whether a battery should charge, discharge, or hold. Every decision has direct profit and loss impact, and we’re already generating 2× revenue for pilot partners.
 What you’ll own
• Forecasting models — design and train the models that predict energy prices, market signals across multiple markets
• Real-time dispatch optimization — build the optimizer that decides battery actions every few minutes, combining ML and stochastic methods under hard physical constraints
• Production ML pipeline — deploy, monitor, and improve models that execute live trades autonomously. Uptime and accuracy matter here as much as research quality.
• Performance infrastructure — build the evaluation layer that tracks model performance against live market outcomes and flags degradation before it costs money
• Product surface — contribute to the frontend (React/TypeScript) that makes forecasts, trades, and performance transparent to customers
 What you’ll get
• You will be building An AI Trader nothing more cool than that — It is one of the most interesting applied ML problems out there
• Equity — you’re a founding engineer, not employee #47. You’ll have real ownership in what we’re building.
• Full technical ownership — you’ll define the ML architecture, choose the tools, and set the direction. No layers of approval.
• Flexible, async work — we care about output, not hours logged. Work from wherever you do your best thinking. (Honest note: You will work a lot)
job_requirements:
What we’re looking for
Must-haves
•      3-7 years Hands-on experience with ML-based optimization — MPC, reinforcement learning, MLIP, stochastic optimization, or similar constrained decision-making problems
•      Background in time-series forecasting and control systems
•      Very strong Mathematical and Modelling skills
•      Strong Python engineering skills and a track record of shipping ML models to production
•      Comfort operating with high autonomy in a small team where you’ll define the technical direction, not just follow it
 Strong advantages
•      Experience with energy markets, electricity pricing, or grid operations
•      Full-stack ability (React / TypeScript) for contributing directly to the product

job_description:
Zealous is a UK-based submissions management platform used by 500+ arts organisations and 180,000+ creators worldwide. Our clients include the Saatchi Gallery, the British Library, and Cass Arts. We handle the end-to-end process for awards, open calls, grants, residencies, and art fairs - all in one place. We've been building for 15 years and we're a team of fourteen.
 This is not a short-term project - if successful you will be a core member of our team.
 We're hiring our first dedicated Product Designer. Until now, design at Zealous has meant UI execution - implementing specs, building components, keeping things consistent. What we haven't had is someone who takes a brief, shapes the flow, prototypes how it should work, and partners with engineering to ship it well. That's this role.
 You'll work directly with our UK-based Founder and PM, who continue shaping product direction and writing briefs. You'll turn those briefs into the designs we build, and work closely with our frontend lead on handoff. Over time - as you build context - we'd like you to grow into user research, building out our design system, and shaping product thinking earlier. You'll be the only designer in the room - your decisions ship directly, without hundreds of layers of review. That's a growth path, not a day-one expectation.
 How we work
Fully remote, Sunday–Thursday. Core hours are 9–5 Egypt time but we're genuinely flexible - several of us work around young kids, and we care about the work getting done well, not clock-watching.
 Day to day responsibilities
Take briefs from the PM and Founder and turn them into well-structured user flows, wireframes, and interactive Figma prototypes
Work closely with our frontend lead on handoff - communicate design intent clearly, answer questions during build, and review what ships against what was designed
Keep our Figma workspace organised and maintainable as it grows - consistent components, sensible naming, files others can find their way around
Sit in design review alongside our frontend lead, flagging feasibility issues and edge cases before things are committed
Raise questions early when a brief is unclear or something doesn't add up - we'd rather hear it upfront than discover it in engineering
 Growth path over 12–18 months:
Run user research and testing sessions directly with organisers, artists, and judges
Build out a lightweight design system our UI developers can work from without asking
Shape product thinking earlier - helping us decide what to build, not just how it should look
 The Offer
£1,300–£1,400/month paid in GBP - stable against local currency fluctuation
Health insurance contribution
21 days paid holiday plus Egyptian public holidays
Work within an Egypt-based team but to the standards of a UK product company
Direct access to the Founder, the PM, and the product roadmap
A clear growth path as you take on more ownership
Work that reaches 500+ arts organisations and 180,000 creators worldwide
 Practical details:
Direct contractor engagement. You'll handle your own tax and social insurance - salary accounts for it, and the team can share their experiences
Use your own laptop to start; we'll provide a company machine after three months
Application to offer within one month
job_requirements:
Must-haves:
3+ years of Product Design or UI/UX Design experience - ideally some time on a SaaS product with more than one type of user
A portfolio showing clean, thoughtful Figma work and shipped product - we care more about clarity of thinking than polish of pixels
Strong Figma craft - components, auto-layout, interactive prototypes, tidy files
Clear written and verbal English - you'll be reporting to a UK-based Founder and most of our collaboration happens async in writing
Comfort working directly with engineers and understanding when a design decision creates a build problem
Self-management - you can run your own time and priorities without being chased
Design for how things work, not just how they look
Willing to raise problems early rather than solve around them quietly
 Bonus points:
Experience running user research or testing (Maze, Useberry, Lookback or similar)
Familiarity with accessibility standards (WCAG)
Exposure to complex form-heavy or multi-role workflows - where getting the logic right matters as much as the layout
A view on where AI tooling is changing design practice, and where it isn't
Care about the creative sector we serve
